title: Sweet Argentinian Pastry Empanadas
description: A sweet and flaky Argentinian pastry filled with raisins, grilled to perfection in a yakimono grill, this empanada is a delightful treat for any time of day, made in a ramekin for a unique vessel.
instructions: Preheat the yakimono grill to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, combine flour, sugar, and cinnamon.
In a separate bowl, whisk together eggs, milk, and vanilla extract.
Add the melted butter to the egg mixture and whisk until combined.
Slowly pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and mix until a dough forms.
Roll out the dough to a thickness of about 1/8 inch.
Cut out circles of dough using a cookie cutter or the rim of a glass.
Place a tablespoon of raisins in the center of each dough circle.
Fold the dough in half over the filling and press the edges together to seal the empanada.
Place the empanadas in a ramekin and grill in the yakimono grill for 10 minutes.
